title: Async-Await: Cooperative Concurrency
overview: A cooperative model where the programmer explicitly marks "waiting" points, allowing the system to switch to other tasks while waiting for I/O.
historical_context: Evolved from callback-hell and promises as a way to manage high-concurrency I/O (Web Explosion), particularly in single-threaded runtimes.
mental_model: Think of a chef who can prepare other ingredients while waiting for the oven to preheat.
key_innovations: Lightweight task switching without full OS thread overhead.
tradeoffs: The "Function Coloring" problem where async functions can only be called from other async contexts.
legacy: Now standard in JavaScript, Python, C#, Rust, and Swift.
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Analyze the implementation of the JavaScript Event Loop vs. Python’s asyncio.
How does Rust’s zero-cost Async differ from other managed implementations?
Explore the history of F#’s Asynchronous Workflows as the precursor to C#’s async/await.
